data.content:
Revised implementation plan for the shared interactive agent session runtime.

Findings
- No knot invariants are currently listed, so the plan is constrained by the acceptance criteria and the workflow boundary only.
- Interactive session behavior is currently split across `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-io.ts` and `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-child.ts`, with duplicated logic for line buffering, JSON parsing, AskUser auto-response handling, stdin close timers, formatted event emission, and close/error cleanup.
- `src/lib/terminal-manager.ts` still owns process-group `SIGTERM`/`SIGKILL` abort behavior separately from the I/O runtimes.
- `src/lib/agent-adapter.ts` currently handles dialect resolution, one-shot arg building, and line normalization for Claude, Codex, Copilot, and OpenCode, but it does not yet expose a shared runtime/capability contract and it does not yet cover Gemini.
- Local characterization so far: `/opt/homebrew/bin/gemini` is installed and `gemini --help` confirms interactive default mode, headless `-p/--prompt`, prompt-interactive `-i/--prompt-interactive`, and `--output-format text|json|stream-json`. Actual Gemini JSON event shape and completion markers still need a characterization step before adapter/runtime behavior is finalized.

Plan
1. Characterize Gemini protocol first via child knot `foolery-ab95`. Capture a minimal local transcript or fixture for `gemini -p ... --output-format stream-json` and, if needed, prompt-interactive mode. Record actual event names, result markers, session-end markers, AskUser-style prompts, whether stdin must remain open for follow-ups, and whether the CLI behaves as interactive, one-shot, or hybrid under the shared capability model.
2. Introduce a shared session capability/runtime layer via a new module family such as `src/lib/agent-session-runtime.ts` and `src/lib/agent-session-capabilities.ts`, or equivalent adjacent files. The shared contract should declare per-agent capabilities for interaction style, prompt transport, follow-up support, AskUser auto-response policy, turn-result detection, session-end detection, stdin drain/close policy, watchdog policy, and post-exit validation.
3. Move duplicated runtime state into that shared layer. The runtime should own line buffering, parsed/normalized event history, auto-answered tool ids, follow-up state, close-input timers, watchdog timers, last result/session-end snapshot, and exit metadata so validators can inspect the final normalized events after `close`/`error`.
4. Centralize runtime operations in shared helpers: parse raw stdout lines, normalize events, emit normalized/ formatted terminal events, auto-answer AskUser, send follow-up prompts, cancel or schedule stdin close, flush trailing buffered output, start/stop watchdog timers, and terminate the child process group with graceful-then-force kill semantics.
5. Refactor both terminal-manager session paths to consume the shared runtime. `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-io.ts` and `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-child.ts` should instantiate the same runtime with different callbacks for event sinks, interaction logging, beat ids, finish behavior, and take-loop close handling, instead of branching on `effectiveDialect === "claude"` or keeping separate copies of the lifecycle code.
6. Evolve the adapter layer so agent definitions provide both spawn configuration and runtime capabilities. `src/lib/agent-adapter.ts` should stop being only a one-shot arg builder/normalizer and instead describe how Claude, Codex, Copilot, OpenCode, and Gemini map into the shared runtime contract. Gemini capability wiring should remain characterization-driven rather than guessed.
7. Wire explicit session-end detection, watchdog lifecycle, and post-exit validation into the live close/abort paths. The runtime should record why a session ended (result observed, session-end event observed, timeout, spawn error, external abort, raw close without result) so `handleTakeIterationClose` and `finishSession` can validate outcomes consistently before retry or completion logic runs.
8. Consolidate abort behavior by moving process-group termination out of `abortSession`-only special handling and into a reusable runtime/process helper that initial and take-loop sessions both rely on. Preserve the current semantics of status transitions, retry behavior, and delayed `SIGKILL` fallback.
9. Expand tests beyond adapter unit coverage. Add runtime/conformance tests that exercise capability declarations, normalized event handling, turn completion detection, session-end detection, watchdog timeout, stdin close/drain behavior, follow-up prompting, and post-exit validation. Add terminal-manager integration coverage for at least one interactive dialect (Claude) and one one-shot dialect (Codex or OpenCode), explicitly covering abort, retry, timeout, and close-input behavior.
10. Likely file touch list:
- `src/lib/agent-adapter.ts`
- new shared runtime/type files under `src/lib/agent-session-*`
- `src/lib/terminal-manager-initial-io.ts`
- `src/lib/terminal-manager-take-child.ts`
- `src/lib/terminal-manager.ts`
- runtime and terminal-manager tests under `src/lib/__tests__/agent-adapter*.test.ts` plus new shared-runtime and integration-focused terminal-manager tests.

Complexity and risks
- Complexity is high because the work cuts across process lifecycle, session completion semantics, retry/abort behavior, and multiple external CLI protocols.
- The primary regression risk is changing subtle differences between the initial-child and take-loop close/error paths, especially around ship follow-up prompting, raw-line fallback, and when session completion is decided.
- The secondary risk is Gemini protocol uncertainty. The safe path is to characterize Gemini first, then encode capabilities from observed behavior rather than inferring them from Claude or Codex.
- Mitigation: land the shared runtime as additive infrastructure, migrate both callers onto it deliberately, and preserve/extend the current abort and retry integration tests while adding explicit conformance coverage.

Child knot hierarchy created
- `foolery-ab95` — Characterize Gemini CLI session protocol
- `foolery-fd80` — Build shared agent session runtime
- `foolery-ac6e` — Adopt shared runtime in terminal manager session paths
- `foolery-e199` — Add agent session runtime conformance coverage
